code: # \donttest{ set.seed(1)  ## Univariate, number of clusters estimated (DPM) y <- c(rnorm(100, -3, 1), rnorm(100, 3, 1)) fit <- nimixClust(y, K_max = 8,                   mcmcControl = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 1000),                   verbose = FALSE) summary(fit) #> Relabelling MCMC output before summarising (label switching)... #> nimix mixture summary (engine: dpm, distribution: normal-uv) #> Observations: 200 (dimension d = 1) #> Relabelling: ECR-ITERATIVE-1 conditioned on modal K = 2 (718 draws) #>  #> Posterior of number of occupied clusters: #>  #>     2     3     4     5     6  #> 0.718 0.246 0.030 0.005 0.001  #>  #> Relabelled component estimates (posterior mean; CIs for univariate): #>  component weight mu_mean mu_med mu_lwr mu_upr s2_mean s2_med s2_lwr s2_upr #>          1  0.501    2.95   2.95   2.73   3.17    1.28   1.26  0.986    1.7 #>          2  0.499   -2.89  -2.89  -3.10  -2.68    1.15   1.14  0.867    1.5 #>  #> Mixing diagnostic (single chain): ESS(#clusters) = 432, ESS(alpha) = 668 #>   Set mcmcControl$nchains > 1 for cross-chain split-Rhat. plot(fit, type = "K")   ## Univariate, fixed number of components (finite mixture) fit2 <- nimixClust(y, K = 2, method = "fixedk",                    mcmcControl = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 1000),                    verbose = FALSE) summary(fit2) #> Relabelling MCMC output before summarising (label switching)... #> Warning: Low effective sample size for the cluster count (ESS = 0 of 1000 draws): the chain may be mixing poorly across partitions. Consider a longer run or k-means initialisation. #> nimix mixture summary (engine: fixedk, distribution: normal-uv) #> Observations: 200 (dimension d = 1) #> Relabelling: ECR-ITERATIVE-1 conditioned on modal K = 2 (1000 draws) #>  #> Posterior of number of occupied clusters: #>  #> 2  #> 1  #>  #> Relabelled component estimates (posterior mean; CIs for univariate): #>  component weight mu_mean mu_med mu_lwr mu_upr s2_mean s2_med s2_lwr s2_upr #>          1  0.499   -2.89  -2.88  -3.10  -2.68    1.15   1.14  0.863   1.53 #>          2  0.501    2.95   2.95   2.73   3.18    1.28   1.26  0.958   1.69 #>  #> Mixing diagnostic (single chain): ESS(#clusters) = 0 #>   Set mcmcControl$nchains > 1 for cross-chain split-Rhat.  ## Multivariate (2-D), DPM Y <- rbind(matrix(rnorm(200, -2), ncol = 2),            matrix(rnorm(200,  2), ncol = 2)) fitMv <- nimixClust(Y, K_max = 8,                     mcmcControl = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 1000),                     verbose = FALSE) summary(fitMv) #> Relabelling MCMC output before summarising (label switching)... #> nimix mixture summary (engine: dpm, distribution: normal-mv) #> Observations: 200 (dimension d = 2) #> Relabelling: ECR-ITERATIVE-1 conditioned on modal K = 3 (303 draws) #>  #> Posterior of number of occupied clusters: #>  #>     2     3     4     5     6     7     8  #> 0.096 0.303 0.298 0.174 0.093 0.024 0.012  #>  #> Relabelled component estimates (posterior mean; CIs for univariate): #>  component weight mu_1_mean mu_1_med mu_1_lwr mu_1_upr mu_2_mean mu_2_med #>          1 0.4705    2.1304   2.1200     1.90     2.45     1.974    1.956 #>          2 0.0898    0.0287   0.0988    -3.03     2.34    -0.549   -0.258 #>          3 0.4397   -2.3393  -2.3235    -2.79    -1.80    -2.047   -2.077 #>  mu_2_lwr mu_2_upr var_1_mean var_1_med var_2_mean var_2_med #>      1.68     2.35      1.032     1.029      0.977     0.977 #>     -3.23     2.33      1.906     1.353      1.627     1.349 #>     -2.33    -1.59      0.944     0.936      1.001     0.996 #>  #> Mixing diagnostic (single chain): ESS(#clusters) = 80, ESS(alpha) = 223 #>   Set mcmcControl$nchains > 1 for cross-chain split-Rhat. plot(fitMv, type = "cluster")  # }

code: fit1 <- nimixReg(y ~ x, df, K = 2, method = "fixedk") fit1 <- relabel(fit1) # residuals of each point under its own MAP component z    <- binderPartition(fit1)$partition cf   <- fit1@relabeled$summary Xm   <- model.matrix(y ~ x, df) res  <- df$y - rowSums(Xm * as.matrix(cf[z, c("(Intercept)", "x")])) s2hat <- sum(res^2) / (nrow(df) - 2 * ncol(Xm))  fit2 <- nimixReg(y ~ x, df, K = 2, method = "fixedk",                  prior = list(s2Guess = s2hat))

code: # \donttest{ set.seed(1) x <- runif(200, -3, 3) grp <- rep(1:2, each = 100) y <- ifelse(grp == 1, 2 * x, -2 * x) + rnorm(200, 0, 0.7) df <- data.frame(y = y, x = x)  ## number of regimes estimated (DPM) fit <- nimixReg(y ~ x, df, K_max = 8,                 mcmcControl = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 1000),                 verbose = FALSE) summary(fit) #> Relabelling MCMC output before summarising (label switching)... #> nimix mixture summary (engine: dpm, distribution: normal-reg) #> Observations: 200 (dimension d = 1) #> Relabelling: ECR-ITERATIVE-1 conditioned on modal K = 2 (834 draws) #>  #> Posterior of number of occupied clusters: #>  #>     2     3     4     5  #> 0.834 0.141 0.024 0.001  #>  #> Relabelled component estimates (posterior mean; CIs for univariate): #>  component weight (Intercept)     x s2_mean s2_med s2_lwr s2_upr #>          1  0.498    -0.06331  1.97   0.993   0.97  0.749   1.33 #>          2  0.502     0.00544 -1.97   1.082   1.06  0.820   1.48 #>  #> Mixing diagnostic (single chain): ESS(#clusters) = 421, ESS(alpha) = 832 #>   Set mcmcControl$nchains > 1 for cross-chain split-Rhat. predict(fit, newdata = data.frame(x = c(-2, 0, 2))) #>    x     .fitted #> 1 -2 -0.01717332 #> 2  0 -0.03112922 #> 3  2 -0.04508512  ## fixed number of regimes (finite mixture) fit2 <- nimixReg(y ~ x, df, K = 2, method = "fixedk",                  mcmcControl = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 1000),                  verbose = FALSE) summary(fit2) #> Relabelling MCMC output before summarising (label switching)... #> Warning: Low effective sample size for the cluster count (ESS = 0 of 1000 draws): the chain may be mixing poorly across partitions. Consider a longer run or k-means initialisation. #> nimix mixture summary (engine: fixedk, distribution: normal-reg) #> Observations: 200 (dimension d = 1) #> Relabelling: ECR-ITERATIVE-1 conditioned on modal K = 2 (1000 draws) #>  #> Posterior of number of occupied clusters: #>  #> 2  #> 1  #>  #> Relabelled component estimates (posterior mean; CIs for univariate): #>  component weight (Intercept)     x s2_mean s2_med s2_lwr s2_upr #>          1  0.496    -0.07053  1.98    1.00  0.988  0.753   1.32 #>          2  0.504    -0.00105 -1.96    1.06  1.055  0.789   1.43 #>  #> Mixing diagnostic (single chain): ESS(#clusters) = 0 #>   Set mcmcControl$nchains > 1 for cross-chain split-Rhat. # }

what: Expected value of the posterior predictive — posteriorEpred
title: Expected value of the posterior predictive — posteriorEpred
text: \(E[Y \mid X]\) posterior draw row data, response scale, averaging components: \(\sum_k w_k \, g^{-1}(x'\beta_k)\), \(g^{-1}\) inverse link family. Normal fit just \(\sum_k w_k x'\beta_k\); Poisson \(\sum_k w_k \exp(x'\beta_k)\), Binomial \(\sum_k w_k \, \mathrm{size} \cdot \mathrm{plogis}(x'\beta_k)\). link applied component average, brms::posterior_epred – averaging first transforming give different, wrong answer.

what: Read this before using it on a mixture
title: Expected value of the posterior predictive — posteriorEpred
text: mixture expectation can describe distribution component . Two crossing lines equal weight flat mixture mean – measured fit, expectation came back 0.009, -0.017 -0.043 x = -1, 0, 1, data whose components slopes +1.5 -1.5. Nothing wrong number; simply summary anyone wants. posteriorLinpred usually right question, regime-switching fit nimixForecast's $regime another. weights posterior allocation probabilities fitted rows. newdata become mixture weights instead, since new row's component unknown – method = "hmm" refused outright, regime weight function time future row decoded regime. Project nimixForecast.

title: Posterior predictive check — ppCheck
text: Simulates replicated data sets fitted mixture – conditionally posterior draw's allocation, makes check label-invariant valid every engine including spatial MRF – compares test statistics replicates observed data via posterior predictive p-values (Gelman, Meng & Stern 1996; Gelman et al. 2020, Section 6). Extreme p-values (near 0 1) flag aspects data model fails reproduce; values near 0.5 indicate evidence misfit statistic.

title: Posterior predictive check — ppCheck
text: fit FitResult nimixClust. nrep Number replicated data sets (posterior draws used), thinned evenly retained draws. Default 200. statistics Named list test-statistic functions, character vector naming built-ins mean, sd, min, max, skew. Multivariate data applies statistic column-wise reports per-column results. seed RNG seed replicate simulation. store_yrep TRUE, attach simulated replicates attribute "yrep" ("y" "draws") result – inputs graphical PPC functions consume.

title: Permute cluster parameters and build the relabelled component summary — relabelComponents
text: relabelComponents(FSSNUvSpec): Permute mu / sigma / alpha, summarise. relabelComponents(FOSSEPUvSpec): Permute mu / sigma / alpha / theta, summarise. relabelComponents(FSSTUvSpec): Permute mu / sigma / alpha / nu, summarise. relabelComponents(PoissonRegSpec): Permute coefficients summarise. relabelComponents(BinomialRegSpec): Permute coefficients summarise. relabelComponents(GMSNBurrUvSpec): Permute mu / sigma / alpha / theta, summarise. relabelComponents(NormalRegSpec): Permute (beta, s2) summarise regression components (one coefficient column per predictor). relabelComponents(JFSTUvSpec): Permute mu / sigma / alpha / theta, summarise. relabelComponents(SEPUvSpec): Permute mu / sigma / nu, summarise. relabelComponents(LEPUvSpec): Permute mu / sigma / nu, summarise. relabelComponents(MSNBurrUvSpec): Permute mu / sigma / alpha summarise. relabelComponents(MSNBurr2aUvSpec): Permute mu / sigma / alpha summarise. relabelComponents(NormalMvRegSpec): Permute coefficient matrices summarise. relabelComponents(NormalMvSpec): Permute multivariate (mu, Sigma) summarise. relabelComponents(NormalUvSpec): Permute (mu, s2) summarise components. relabelComponents(PoissonSpec): Permute lambda summarise. relabelComponents(BinomialSpec): Permute prob summarise. relabelComponents(SkewNormalMvSpec): Permute mv (mu, Sigma, gamma) summarise. relabelComponents(SkewNormalMvOSpec): Permute mv params plus theta, summarise. relabelComponents(SkewIStudentMvSpec): Permute mv (mu, Sigma, gamma, nu), summarise. relabelComponents(SkewIStudentMvOSpec): Permute mv params plus nu theta. relabelComponents(SkewNormalMvOGenSpec): Permute components, canonicalise draw's orthogonal factor via FS restriction (8), adjusting gamma . relabelComponents(SkewIStudentMvOGenSpec): Permute components, canonicalise draw's orthogonal factor, carrying gamma nu permutation. relabelComponents(StudentTUvSpec): Permute (mu, tau) summarise; reported scale \(\sigma = \tau^{-1/2}\).

title: Draw a response given linear predictors and error scale — responseRng
text: responseRng(DistributionSpec): Gaussian: identity link, Normal noise. responseRng(PoissonRegSpec): Poisson: log link, Poisson draw. responseRng(BinomialRegSpec): Binomial: logit link, Binomial draw. responseRng(StudentTRegSpec): Student-t regression: identity link, scaled-t noise. direct Student-t Normal-Gamma augmentation Student-t marginal, share . s2 error VARIANCE, t df degrees freedom \(\sigma^2 \, \mathrm{df}/(\mathrm{df}-2)\); draw \(\eta + \sigma \, t_{\mathrm{df}}\) \(\sigma = \sqrt{s2 (\mathrm{df}-2)/\mathrm{df}}\). responseRng(NormalGammaRegSpec): Normal-Gamma regression: Student-t marginal direct parameterisation, scaled-t draw. Defined explicitly two heavy-tail specs siblings NormalRegSpec, parent child – inheritance give Gaussian default silently drop tails.

title: Subbotin Exponential Power (SEP) Distribution — sep-distribution
text: x, q Numeric vector quantiles. mu Numeric. Location parameter (default = 0). sigma Numeric. Scale parameter, must positive (default = 1). nu Numeric. TRUE, probabilities/densities given log. Default FALSE. lower.tail Logical. TRUE (default), probabilities \(P[X \le x]\), otherwise \(P[X > x]\). p Numeric vector probabilities. n Integer. Number observations generate.

u}\,   \Gamma(1/
u)\,\sigma}   \exp\left(- rac{1}{2}\left| rac{x-\mu}{\sigma}ight|^
uight)$$ \(-\infty < x < \infty\), \(-\infty < \mu < \infty\), \(\sigma > 0\), \(
u > 0\). SEP symmetric around \(\mu\). Special cases: \(
u = 2\): Normal distribution \(\mathcal{N}(\mu, \sigma^2)\) \(
u = 1\): Laplace (double exponential) distribution \(
u \infty\): Uniform distribution (limit)

text: SpatialWeightSpec   \(n_{loc} \times n_{time}\) nodes.

title: Build a space-time adjacency for spatio-temporal mixtures — spacetimeAdjacency
text: Observations must ordered time varying slowest – node \((, t)\) row \((t - 1) n_{loc} + \) – .vector() \(n_{loc} \times n_{time}\) matrix gives. 5x5 grid 8 time points deliberately overlapping components, plain mixture failed outright (allocation accuracy 0.50, one component recovered); spatial graph reached 0.95; space-time graph reached 0.995. temporal edges earn place. Note coupling isotropic: one beta governs spatial temporal edges alike, Potts prior reads adjacency unweighted. Misspecifying mild rather fatal – regimes random across space perfectly persistent time, imposing spatial edges anyway cost 2.5 percentage points (0.900 0.925 temporal-graph). structure purely temporal, build temporal-graph spatial = FALSE. pure time series spatial component, method = "hmm" better tool: marginalises state path (better mixing) offers viterbiPath. function space matters .

title: Build a space-time adjacency for spatio-temporal mixtures — spacetimeAdjacency
text: graph longer limits problem size: SpatialWeightSpec stores edge list, 10 000-node space-time graph builds fraction second within tens MB (OOM-killed outright v1.5.0, construction), 50 000 nodes take second. binding constraint now NIMBLE's per-node model memory nimbleModel/compilation: measured 4 GB container, fits ran 5000 nodes (~1.6 GB) 7000 nodes (~2.8 GB) died near 10 000. need , RAM buys roughly linearly – graph longer wall.

what: ESS benchmark for regression across engines
title: nimix 1.5.0
text: Documented effective-sample-size cost non-Gaussian regressions. Gaussian conjugate coefficient update; measured fixed-K two components, Gaussian reaches ~10.7 ESS/s 1.9 MSNBurr 1.4 three-shape GMSNBurr (5-7x factor). HMM slower still per second fixed-K (regime path marginalised, coefficients correlated), DPM mixes well concentration parameter. ?nimixReg now states factor users can budget iterations; full benchmark write-ships project notes.

what: Random effects extend to the DPM engine (and a note on HMM)
title: nimix 1.5.0
text: Neo-normal (Gaussian) regressions accept random effects DPM engine : nimixReg(y ~ x, method = "dpm", distribution = "msnburr", random = ~ grp). random-cluster allocation fixed external grouping act iid exchangeable observations, offsets sit cleanly location, fixed-K. Measured: msnburr cor(b_hat, truth) = 0.96, sep 1.00, gmsnburr 1.00. Random effects remain refused HMM engine, now clear reason: single time series exchangeable grouping, offset unidentified confounds regime transitions. Panel-HMM (multiple series) prerequisite. See design note full argument.

what: Random effects for GLM regression – the genuine GLMM case
title: nimix 1.5.0
text: Poisson Binomial regression now accept group-level random effects: nimixReg(y ~ x, distribution = "poisson", random = ~ grp), ~ x | grp random slope. Unlike location-scale families, GLM random effect enters INSIDE link (log logit), group offset multiplicative response scale – true GLMM design study flagged. tauRE therefore lives link scale, prior bounds fixed rather scaled sd(y) (large count otherwise give absurd ceiling). Measured six groups: Poisson cor(b_hat, truth) = 0.96, Binomial 0.99; Poisson random slope 0.93/0.99. Fixed-K engine.

what: Random effects for MSNBurr regression (F10 prototype)
title: nimix 1.5.0
text: MSNBurr regression accepts group-level random effects, like Gaussian Student-t regressions: nimixReg(y ~ x, distribution = "msnburr", random = ~ grp) random intercept, random = ~ x | grp add random slope. neo-normal families location-scale, random effect enters location exactly Gaussian (unlike GLM, act link), sum--zero parameterisation. Measured six groups: random-intercept recovery cor(b_hat, truth) = 0.999; random slope , 0.98 1.00. Fixed-K engine. nine neo-normal families now support random effects generic path (.neoRegREConstants + re/reSlope arguments .neoRegFixedKCode); measured recovery cor(b_hat, truth) >= 0.98 across families.

what: Neo-normal regression completes the engine trio with HMM
title: nimix 1.5.0
text: nine neo-normal regression families now run HMM engine – nimixReg(y ~ x, method = "hmm", distribution = "msnburr") rest – giving Markov-switching skewed / heavy-tailed regression. per-state location linear predictor, scale shape switching regime. Measured two regimes: MSNBurr intercepts -1.49/1.53 -1.5/1.5. forward FFBS kernel, BUGSdist registration, three S4 methods (model code, allocation density, forecast RNG) generated two facts – family’s density kernel shape list – via .makeNeoHMMRegKernel / .makeNeoHMMRegMethods. hand-written nimbleFunction per family. Together fixed-K DPM variants, every neo-normal family now spans full engine trio.

what: Neo-normal regression now runs under the DPM (nonparametric) engine
title: nimix 1.5.0
text: Every neo-normal regression family gains Dirichlet-process variant – nimixReg(y ~ x, method = "dpm", distribution = "msnburr") eight – number components inferred rather fixed. DPM model code generated generically family’s density name shape-prior lines (ingredients fixed-K code uses), per-family DPM code written. conjugate cluster wrapper (likelihood Gaussian coefficients); NIMBLE samples cluster parameters directly.

what: Prediction for neo-normal regression is now complete
title: nimix 1.5.0
text: posteriorLinpred() posteriorEpred() already worked neo-normal regressions (identity link), posteriorPredictive() fell back Gaussian draw lost skew. now draws family via generic responseRng framework attaches family’s RNG – MSNBurr predictive skewed, FSST one heavy-tailed, . Works across shape counts (2 3 parameters); shape traces pulled name small registry keyed family.

what: New: neo-normal regression (framework + MSNBurr and SEP)
title: nimix 1.5.0
text: Skewed heavy-tailed regression: neo-normal component’s location linear predictor X beta_k, shape parameters staying per-component. nimixReg(y ~ x, K = 2, distribution = "msnburr") "sep", , "msnburr2a" – now nine neo-normal families: msnburr, msnburr2a, gmsnburr, fssn, fsst, sep, lep, fossep, jfst. Shape sets range two (sigma, alpha sigma, nu) three (sigma, alpha, theta), Gamma, log-normal, truncated-Gamma shape priors; framework absorbs – new family short declaration (density, shape list, prior), traces, relabelling, summary columns follow shape list automatically. Measured two components: MSNBurr intercepts -1.44/1.64 -1.5/1.5; SEP intercepts -1.26/1.10 -1.2/1.2. Built generic helper (.neoRegMethods) new family needs density, list shape parameters, short prior – six boilerplate S4 methods (traces, relabelling, simulation, …) generated shape list. MSNBurr MSNBurr-IIa use (sigma, alpha), SEP uses (sigma, nu); summary columns follow automatically (sigma_mean + family’s shape means). remaining seven neo-normal families can added way. conjugate sampler (likelihood Gaussian coefficients), budget iterations, non-Gaussian regressions.

what: New: Markov-switching heavy-tail regression (Student-t and Normal-Gamma)
title: nimix 1.5.0
text: nimixReg(y ~ x, K = 2, method = "hmm", distribution = "studentt") ("normalgamma") fits regression whose location coefficients scale switch latent Markov regime, Student-t errors – heavy-tail sibling Gaussian, Poisson, Binomial Markov-switching regressions. forward kernel (dt_nonstandard, df fixed hyperparameter), inherited Gaussian one: guard refuses inheritance (silent-wrong kernel otherwise) dedicated kernel runs instead. Measured two regimes df = 4 errors: intercepts -1.50/1.45 -1.5/1.5, slopes -0.88/1.26 -0.9/1.2, decoding 0.98. "normalgamma" shares kernel (identical Student-t marginal) buildModelCode method, two heavy-tail specs siblings NormalRegSpec, parent child – inheritance handed Gaussian default. nimixForecast() handles : predictive draws keep heavy tails. closes long-standing backlog item; HMM regression families now normal, studentt, normalgamma, poisson, binomial (18 HMM kernels).

title: nimix 1.5.0
text: third cluster-seeding option alongside "kmeans" (default) "single". univariate response, "spread" bands observations |y - median(y)|, separating components scale rather location. targets one case measured seed k-means poorly: components different variances overlapping means (e.g. sd 0.3 vs 3, centred near zero), k-means forced draw spatial boundary cuts across real, scale-based split. Initial-allocation accuracy case rises 0.84 (k-means) 0.90. convenience, cure. three initialisation studies found, well-mixed Bayesian mixture recovers poor seed – MCMC result whether seeded "kmeans" "spread". value "spread" shorter burn-heterogeneous-variance case, different answer. multivariate response natural analogue falls back k-means. Shared internally via single .initClusters() helper, future seeding strategies written rather family’s componentInits.

what: New: Markov-switching GLM regression (Poisson and Binomial)
title: nimix 1.5.0
text: nimixReg(y ~ x, K = 2, method = "hmm", distribution = "poisson") fits count regression whose log-rate coefficients switch latent Markov regime – count sibling Gaussian Markov-switching regression. forward kernel (dpois(exp(Xbeta)), log link, error variance), inherited one. Measured two regimes: intercepts 0.93/2.23 1.0/2.2, slopes 0.84/-0.51 0.8/-0.5, decoding 0.967. distribution = "binomial" logit link known number trials (prior = list(size = )): proportion regression regime-switching coefficients. Measured: intercepts -1.03/1.50 -1.0/1.5, slopes 1.22/-0.81 1.2/-0.8, decoding 0.997. nimixForecast() handles : predictive draws counts (Poisson) proportions size (Binomial). Fixed alongside: forecaster tested (spec, "NormalRegSpec") decide whether fit covariates, missed sibling GLM specs; now tests shared regression trait.

what: Fixed: posteriorEpred()/posteriorPredictive() now respect the link
title: nimix 1.5.0
text: first cut brms-style predictors Gaussian-disguise: posteriorEpred() averaged linear predictors x'beta_k directly, posteriorPredictive() added Gaussian noise. Poisson (log link) Binomial (logit link) regression simply wrong – epred returned log-mean instead mean, predictive drew Gaussian jitter instead counts. Now matched brms. posteriorEpred() E[Y|X] response scale, applying family’s inverse link every component mixture average: sum_k w_k g^{-1}(x'beta_k). Verified Poisson fit – epred came back 0.87/1.79/3.68 truth exp(0.5 + 0.8x) = 0.74/1.65/3.67, linear predictor 0.5 + 0.8x. posteriorLinpred() stays linear-predictor scale design (log-mean Poisson), gains transform argument – counterpart brms’s – apply inverse link want component’s response mean instead. Normal fit two coincide, -op . posteriorPredictive() now draws family via new responseRng() generic, reaches every univariate regression family, just Gaussian one. Poisson predictive draw count, Binomial one number successes, Student-t Normal-Gamma one heavy-tailed – first cut drew Gaussian noise , giving predictive measured kurtosis 3.14 t df = 3 far heavier; now 5.0 (Student-t) 7.1 (Normal-Gamma). epred already right, since use identity link E[Y|X] = Xbeta; predictive law wrong. Multivariate-response fits unaffected – posteriorLinpred() friends decline design, predict() handles case. Fixed alongside: K = 1 regression predicted newdata (mixture weight node); now takes weight one.

what: brms-style prediction, time-series views, and Markov autoregression
title: nimix 1.5.0
text: Three prediction functions, mixture makes three different questions. posteriorLinpred() returns component’s linear predictor (draws x n x K array); posteriorEpred() averages components E[Y|X]; posteriorPredictive() adds residual noise. mixture, reach first far often second – fit two crossing lines slopes +1.5 -1.5 expectation flat line middle (measured 0.028/0.069/0.111 x = -1/0/1). Nothing wrong number; faithful summary distribution component . ?posteriorEpred says . weights change meaning calls, docs explicit : -sample, posteriorEpred() uses row’s posterior allocation becomes useful (measured correlation 0.983 y fit whose newdata expectation flat); newdata uses mixture weights, since new row’s component unknown. method = "hmm" newdata refused outright: regime weight function , future row decoded regime – nimixForecast() function projects . Fixed: silent wrong answer prediction newdata. newdata lacked predictor, model.frame() resolved name formula’s environment – fitted data – returned predictions original rows. Measured fix: one row newdata , 10 x 60 x 2 array . predict() surfaced downstream “replacement 60 rows, data 1”; paths now check front name missing predictors. Time-series views regime-switching fits: plot(fit, type = "series") draws data decoded regimes shaded blocks, type = "regime" smoothed regime probabilities time (honest companion Viterbi path – bands mixed, decode guess), type = "forecast" predictive fan. density plot switching series discards axis model ; put back. returns tidy frame drew, plot types . Markov-switching autoregression works today, new code: lag response design matrix. nimixReg(y ~ ylag, df, K = 2, method = "hmm") fits MS-AR(1) whose intercept AR coefficient switch regime – measured, intercepts -1.42/1.48 -1.5/1.5 AR coefficients 0.76/0.32 0.75/0.30, decoding 1.0. coincidence implementation: conditioning lagged y standard conditional likelihood AR model, forward kernel takes arbitrary design matrix. One practical note now measured: "hmm" regression path needs iterations "fixedk". Marginalising regime path rules conjugate sampler, NIMBLE’s defaults mix coefficients four times slower per second (ESS/s 2.3 vs 8.9 two-regime benchmark), even though wall time shorter. Light runs decode regimes fine leave coefficient intervals wide; ?nimixReg says . forecasting one now works , via nimixForecast(fit, h, lags = c(ylag = 1)). lags marks predictors response disguise; columns generated forecast, supplied , posterior draw feeding trajectory back lag. makes interval widen way autoregression’s – measured MS-AR(1), 2.15 h = 1 5.12 h = 10, RMSE 0.54 3.97 constant-mean benchmark. Exogenous predictors go newdata lags lags; give predictor one , never (measured MS-ARX: RMSE 0.83 5.29). fake lag newdata. taken known covariate, held fixed, forecast inherit whatever invented – confidently without complaint, nothing design matrix says “column past”. lags exists , ?nimixForecast says read.

title: nimix 1.5.0
text: nimixForecast(fit, h) draws posterior predictive h steps past end series, integrating parameter uncertainty (one path per posterior draw) regime uncertainty (regime sampled, fixed). forward algorithm run one step : filter regime distribution last observation, push transition matrix h times, sample regime, draw regime’s emission. 13 emission families supported, plus Markov-switching regression (pass newdata future covariates – regime can projected forward, predictors ). Returns $summary (mean/median/interval), $regime (h x K matrix regime probabilities), $draws (raw predictive sample). Calibration measured, asserted: interval coverage 0.917 nominal 0.90 12-step horizon two-regime benchmark, RMSE 1.93 3.23 constant-mean forecast. Three honest limits, documented ?nimixForecast pinned tests. (1) point forecast reverts stationary mixture h grows – measured, median fell 1.8 -1.1 twelve steps regime probabilities went 0.11/0.89 0.59/0.41, converging stationary 0.67/0.33. model working: HMM forecasts regime, regime unknowable best guess long-run average. (2) Accuracy hinges whether regime persists: Markov-switching regression held regime across boundary forecast beat benchmark fivefold (RMSE 0.50 vs 2.65); one switched exactly first forecast step – 5% event fitted persistence – lost (4.42 vs 2.45), interval still covered. switch one step ahead unpredictable construction. (3) separated regimes predictive bimodal, median sits trough modes model puts almost mass; $summary convenient, $draws faithful, $regime usually answers question actually asked.

title: nimix 1.5.0
text: model, one classic mixture--regressions variant nimix missing. Give K regimes "fixedk"; regime path marginalised forward kernel decoded afterwards, viterbiPath() returns probable regime sequence. Measured two regimes opposite slopes: intercepts 1.96/-1.96 2/-2, slopes 1.50/-1.39 1.5/-1.5, decoding 0.98. Unlike every emission, one’s density depends t design matrix rather y[t]; kernel carries X. Note rows time series – order matters, true nowhere else nimixReg. conjugate sampler , requirement. Normal-Inverse-Gamma update conditions allocations, HMM marginalises away, coefficient full conditional longer NIG NIMBLE’s defaults used instead. Installing conjugate sampler anyway exactly silent-wrong-sampler bug fixed Student-t regression 1.4.0; test now asserts mechanism, just outcome. "studentt" "normalgamma" refused now. merely unimplemented: contains Gaussian regression spec inherit new HMM model code, handed user asking heavy tails plain Gaussian fit error. guard matches exact class rather (), test pins . usual caveat default error-variance prior applies force : regimes opposing slopes inflate global OLS residual variance prior centred (measured s2 ~2.2x benchmark ). prior = list(s2Guess = ) escape hatch, regression paths.

Measuring first turned unify two earlier findings rather repeat either. mechanism univariate regression one: multivariate prior centres Sigma cov() global OLS residuals, carry -component variation well within. two components differing coefficients, isotropic within-covariance, prior 22.4x truth trace condition number 40 truth circle – wrong size wrong shape. severity multivariate one: fitted covariance came back ~1.5x condition ~2, df0 = d + 2 keeps InverseWishart prior worth exactly one observation univariate InvGamma worth four. error, four times less weight, shows. default therefore untouched, clustering case. Reach sigmaGuess = s know residual scale isotropic variance s, pass d x d matrix. Every number anchored test.

what: HMM emissions complete: 13 families, and a silent-dispatch bug fixed
title: nimix 1.5.0
text: Four emission families method = "hmm": sep, lep (exponential-power, whose tail-shape nu proved well identified – measured 1.30/3.48 truth 1.2/3.5), four-parameter skew families fossep jfst, binomial (regime-switching proportions known size, via prior = list(size = ); measured recovery prob 0.150/0.597 0.15/0.6, Viterbi 1.0). Measured decoding simulated regimes: Viterbi 0.993 (sep), 1.0 (lep, fossep, jfst). JFST’s alpha theta jointly govern skew tails weakly identified, like FSST’s df – documented asserted loosely, per precedent. completes planned univariate set 12 families; normal-gamma excluded design, since augmented representation exactly marginalised forward kernel exists avoid, direct student-t serves heavy-tail case. Fixed: silent wrong-model dispatch. NormalGammaUvSpec inherits NormalUvSpec, engine’s ()-based family guard accepted distribution = "normal-gamma" S4 dispatch fell inherited Gaussian HMM method: user asked heavy tails got plain normal fit, error, ever since HMM engine existed. guard now matches exact class. Found new permanent guard test moment moved onto normal-gamma – relocated four times rollout advanced, now sits family excluded forever, can never go stale .

what: Sparse adjacency: the graph is no longer the wall
title: nimix 1.5.0
text: SpatialWeightSpec now stores graph edge list; dense matrix derived demand (getAdjacency()) refused 5000 nodes message pointing new getEdges(). motivation measured, assumed: 10 000-node space-time graph (100 locations 100 times – modest spatio-temporal problem) OOM-killed graph construction, model existed, quantities Potts engine actually needs came ~0.6 MB, 2700x smaller dense path’s transients. refactor graph builds quarter second within ~40 MB, 50 000-node graph second. Nothing changes numerically. edge list kept exactly column-major order (upper.tri() & > 0) produced old dense path, engine’s structural constants – therefore fits fixed seed – bit-identical; test suite asserts equivalence rook queen grids space-time graphs. spatialWeights() dense matrix still works, validation error messages, getAdjacency() still returns named matrix small graphs. New sparse entry points: spatialWeights(edges = , nNodes = ) builds graph without ever touching dense matrix (edge order duplicates normalised), getEdges() returns canonical two-column form. gridAdjacency() spacetimeAdjacency() now build edge lists directly. One incidental find chasing memory gate: duplicated() matrix coerces every row character string – 55 MB transient 28 000-edge graph, twice inside validity. Edges now deduplicated numeric key (- 1) * n + j, exact double realistic n. honest ledger. fit ceiling 4 GB container rose ~5000 ~7000 nodes (57 s, allocation accuracy 0.999 7000); 10 000 fit still dies, binding constraint now NIMBLE’s per-node model memory (~1.6 GB 5000, ~2.8 GB 7000), graph. RAM now buys nodes roughly linearly, true . Breaking change: SpatialWeightSpec objects serialised (saveRDS) earlier versions loaded; rebuild adjacency edges. Code used exported constructors accessors unaffected.

title: nimix 1.4.0
text: spacetimeAdjacency(W, nTime) expands spatial adjacency time, node (, t) neighbours spatial neighbours t t +/- 1. Pass result nimixClust(..., method = "mrf") Potts prior couples allocations across space time. new engine: architectural study behind found long-standing blocker – “undirected spatial dependence breaks HMM forward algorithm” – true beside point, since MRF engine never used forward algorithm; samples allocations directly. space-time graph simply another adjacency. Measured 5x5 grid 8 time points deliberately overlapping components: plain mixture failed outright (allocation accuracy 0.50, one component recovered), spatial graph reached 0.95, space-time graph 0.995 – temporal edges earn place rather merely decorating. One honest limit: coupling isotropic, Potts prior reads adjacency unweighted, single beta governs spatial temporal edges alike. Misspecifying mild rather fatal – regimes random across space perfectly persistent time, imposing spatial edges anyway cost 2.5 percentage points (0.900 0.925 temporal-graph, spatial = FALSE builds). pure time series, method = "hmm" remains better tool: marginalises state path offers viterbiPath(). two complementary – hmm time alone, space-time Potts space matters .

what: New: random slopes in the mixture of regressions
title: nimix 1.4.0
text: nimixReg(y ~ x, data, random = ~ x | group) adds group-varying slope x top random intercept (lme4’s (x|g), intercept comes along); random = ~ group still gives intercept alone. gate prototype tested obvious worry – group-varying slopes fight component identification, since components mixture regressions distinguished slopes – materialise: even group slope deviations spanning -2.05 1.43 component separation 4 (one group’s effective slope sat exactly two components), group slopes recovered cor = 0.997 component allocation 95.3% accuracy. offsets use sum--zero parameterisation independent priors. gate measured : free offsets produce two translation ridges (cor(beta1, mean(s)) = -0.929, cor(beta0, mean(b)) = -0.953) min ESS 52, 226 constraint – notably free version received conjugate samplers NIMBLE still lost, .e. posterior geometry beats sampler class. Independence checked correlated truth (rho = 0.92): independent model recovered sets offsets (cor = 0.97) correlation reappeared empirically (cor(b_hat, s_hat) = 0.876), correlated prior buys little moderate group sizes. sparse groups matter . Semantics, intercept: component slopes absorb mean(s_g) reported sRE centred. tauSlope estimates spread groups’ slopes. Random effects (intercept slope) now also work heavy-tailed residuals: distribution = "studentt" accepts random = ~ group random = ~ x | group. needed sampler work, family keeps NIMBLE’s default samplers (see dispatch fix ) – offsets simply enter linear predictor. Measured simulated t4 residuals: slopes -1.99/1.95 cor(b_hat, centred truth) = 0.992 intercept, -1.82/2.09 sum--zero prediction -1.88/2.12 cor = 0.961 slope.

what: The prior scale on the component error variance is now settable, and documented
title: nimix 1.4.0
text: defaultPrior centres InvGamma prior s2 residual variance global OLS fit, ignores mixture. separated components measures spread components much spread within one, s2 biased upward – bias largest exactly mixtures useful. study measured : prior/truth scale ratio near 60 bias runs ~9x 25 observations per component, 2.5x 150, 1.2x 1000, 1.0x 5000; component separation n = 150 runs 1.0x overlapping components 4.7x well-separated ones. prior’s six nominal pseudo-observations carry variance ~39x truth, makes worth roughly 236 real ones. default change, conservatism turned load-bearing rather incidental: shrinking prior scale 10x left DPM’s K recovery untouched (modal K = 2 throughout) made -specified fixedk fit (K = 4 2 true components) occupy three components instead two. Wide components make two suffice; narrow ones make model want . bias also safe direction (wider predictive intervals), coefficients unaffected either way – stayed invisible. new escape hatch. g nu0 overridable scale , user knew within-component variance way say . Now prior = list(s2Guess = 0.3) makes 0.3 prior mean s2; distribution = "studentt" inherits , s0 still sets raw InvGamma scale callers think terms. benchmark (true s2 = 0.25) moved estimate 0.78 (3.1x) 0.36 (1.4x), slopes unchanged. override deliberately absolute: relative multiplier automatic scale considered dropped, since “tenth quantity measures wrong thing” knob, statement user can defend. tighter prior safe documented tested sides: safe DPM fixedk correct K (halved error s2 cost), unsafe fixedk -specified K, tenfold tighter prior occupied three components default’s two. ?nimixReg gains “Reading error variance” section numbers, boundary, two-stage empirical-Bayes recipe callers need scale right without external knowledge: first fit’s allocation reliable even s2 , can supply s2Guess second fit. Every number anchored test.

what: The multivariate prior ellipse: measured, kept, and now overridable
title: nimix 1.4.0
text: multivariate analogue scale study turned different failure. cov(data) global covariance, separated components inflated along direction separates : measured isotropic components separated along vector v, 37.6x along v 0.9x across – prior ellipse condition number 42.8 truth circle. prior wrong shape, merely wrong size. yet default survives , unlike univariate one: fitted covariance came back 1.2x condition 1.4. reason happy piece existing design – df0 = d + 2 makes InverseWishart prior worth exactly one observation, every d, univariate nu0 = 3 worth four. multivariate bias therefore milder constant dimension (1.18x 200 observations per component d), opposite one might expect. univariate copy trick: weight floored two observations nu0 > 2 requirement keeps empty components finite. default express shape, prior = list(sigmaGuess = s) now sets prior mean component’s covariance directly – positive scalar reads isotropic, pass d x d positive-definite matrix. benchmark sigmaGuess = 0.25 restored prior ellipse circle (condition 42.8 1). default untouched; numbers anchored tests.

what: Fixed: Student-t regression was using a Gaussian-error sampler (affects earlier versions)
title: nimix 1.4.0
text: StudentTRegSpec inherits NormalRegSpec, S4 dispatch handed NormalRegSpec’s customizeSamplers, installs exact Normal-Inverse-Gamma Gibbs step (betaTilde, s2Tilde). conditional exact Gaussian likelihood: distribution = "studentt" drew wrong conditional, accept/reject correct , chain targeted wrong stationary distribution. affected method = "fixedk" (engine whose allocation node z); DPM path never affected, since allocation node xi method returned early. symptom easy miss: slopes barely touched (symmetric errors), scale moved. Measured correct RW_block reference model data, s2 biased ~17% df = 4 (0.99/1.08 vs 0.84/0.91, MCSE 0.003), gap shrank ~1% df = 30 – bias vanishes t approaches Normal, pins mechanism likelihood mismatch rather Monte Carlo error. StudentTRegSpec now keeps NIMBLE’s default samplers, correct (verified: s2 0.84/0.91, matching reference; ESS 580 coefficients 1010 scale). normal-reg normal-gamma-reg unaffected – use , correct samplers, test suite now asserts dispatch three.

what: Fixed: random-effect priors are now data-scaled (affects 1.3.0)
title: nimix 1.4.0
text: random-intercept prior shipped 1.3.0 used fixed bounds dunif(0.01, 5) tauRE, silently broke offsets whenever response large scale: y multiplied 1000 needed tauRE 771 hard ceiling 5, cor(b_hat, truth) collapsed 0.992 0.091 – error, warning, just wrong group effects. bounds now scale data like rest nimix’s priors (tauRE sd(y); tauSlope sd(y)/sd(x)), inits scale . Verified across 1000x response rescale (cor(b_hat, truth) 0.991, tauRE 817 realized spread 772) 1000x predictor rescale (tauSlope 0.000274 realized 0.00029, previously pinned 0.01 floor). existing scale-equivariance lock never caught rescales predictors . response-rescaling test now closes gap, new test-prior-scale-invariance.R locks invariant per engine. audit remaining priors found violation: fixedk, dpm mrf engines exactly scale-equivariant response (conjugate samplers reproduce draws data-scaled priors), hmm agrees within Monte Carlo error. one caveat worth knowing hmm large-scale data: adaptive random-walk samplers start NIMBLE’s default proposal scale 1 regardless data, short chains show transient adaptation difference (0.08 2500 iterations, 0.001 MCSE 0.0013 8000) – allow adequate burnin rather reading short chain bias.

title: nimix 1.3.0
text: release adds fourth inference engine – hidden-Markov mixtures regime switching time series, six emission families – random intercepts grouped data mixture regressions, delivered gated prototypes whose measured findings recorded .

what: New: random intercepts in the mixture of regressions
title: nimix 1.3.0
text: nimixReg(y ~ x, data, random = ~ group, method = "fixedk") adds shared group offset b_g ~ N(0, tau^2) every component’s linear predictor. Two design decisions came measured gate prototype baked : () sum--zero parameterisation – free b, component intercepts mean(b) form pure translation ridge (cor = -0.979, min ESS 25 2500); constraint restored min ESS 191-238 recovery intact (cor(b_hat, centred truth) = 0.992, tau_hat 0.83 realized group spread 0.77), reported b centred component intercepts absorbing group mean. Note tauRE estimates spread groups: groups realized spread variable (12 groups drawn sigma = 0.8 spans roughly 0.5-1.1), judge tau_hat groups , population sigma may simulated . (ii) exact NIG Gibbs sampler gains random-effect offset: gate found NIMBLE’s conjugacy detection handle dynamic indexing additive scalar form, inprod form production uses – P1 sampler now conditions current b. P1 scale-equivariance lock still holds RE active (measured slope discrepancy 0.0026 1000x rescale), test suite asserts . Scope: method = "fixedk" distribution = "normal", one grouping factor, random intercept. Random slopes families follow gated plan; combinations refused pointed message.

what: New engine: hidden-Markov mixtures (regime switching), method = "hmm"
title: nimix 1.3.0
text: Component labels can now follow first-order Markov chain time: nimixClust(y, K = S, method = "hmm") fits regime-switching mixture state path marginalised likelihood forward algorithm – MCMC ever samples continuous parameters. gate prototype measured : 4000 iterations ~1 s T = 300, min ESS/sec 456 vs 144 naive latent-state model (x3.2 easy setting, marginalised model removes T discrete nodes graph). forward kernel exact pure-R reference (difference 0, compiled uncompiled), asserted test suite. Allocation draws recovered post-hoc forward-filter backward-sampling (FFBS) per retained draw, every existing tool works unchanged HMM fits: relabel(), psm(), binderPartition(), plot(), bayesplot adaptors. Measured simulated regimes: location recovery -1.99/2.14 (truth -2/2), self-transitions 0.965/0.895 (truth 0.95/0.90), per-time MAP Viterbi decoding accuracy 1.0. viterbiPath(fit) returns jointly probable state sequence posterior means – complementary binderPartition(), summarises marginal co-clustering across FFBS draws. nimbleEcology evaluated used: dHMM family categorical-emission , regime switching continuous data needs continuous emissions. gate showed nimix’s kernels compile exactly inside forward pass, path extending engine emission families – current scope "normal", "student-t", "poisson" (count regimes), neo-normal skewed families "msnburr", "msnburr2a", "gmsnburr", "fssn", "fsst" – eight families, univariate. Measured recovery simulated regimes: FSSN mu -3.06/3.27 skew alpha 0.63/1.54, FSST (skewed heavy-tailed ) mu -4.05/4.18 alpha 0.69/1.51, decoding Viterbi 1.0. FSST’s degrees freedom deliberately asserted tightly: nu weakly identified tails moderate (run gave 12.8/13.3 truth 6 everything else landed), property family rather defect. New emission families implement one density method (.hmmEmisDens) one forward kernel; engine, FFBS, viterbiPath() family-generic, Poisson case (non-location-scale emission) confirms. FFBS allocation decoding Viterbi now numerically hardened emission underflow: thin-tailed family outlying point can drive every state’s density 0 draw, previously produced NaN weights “NA probability vector” crash. forward pass now falls back uniform states degenerate case, Viterbi floors zero densities taking logs – decoding stays correct normal regimes longer crashes outliers (asserted dedicated test). -parameterised fits (nStates true number regimes) leave empty states without corruption, asserted dedicated test (structural lesson 1.2.0 PPC bug applied new engine day one).

title: nimix 1.2.1
text: clusterValidity(fit) computes silhouette width, Dunn index, Calinski-Harabasz clustering fit’s point partition (default: binderPartition(), every posterior draw contributes relabelling needed), via cluster fpc packages – Suggests. documentation states, test suite asserts, honest caveat: indices reward geometric separation, mixtures density-based – overlapping components can exactly right model still score low (measured: silhouette 0.90/Dunn 1.6 separated clusters vs 0.52/0.0 legitimate overlapping fit). secondary comparison lens; ppCheck() psm() remain primary model-adequacy tools.

title: nimix 1.2.0
text: Response wave external code review: two correctness fixes, five new exported functions, sampler-default upgrades, test-harness overhaul. Every claim measured -session; see file headers numbers’ provenance.

what: Improved: plots return their data; PPC column lookup memoised
title: nimix 1.2.0
text: Every plot(fit, type = ...) branch now returns, invisibly, tidy data frame drew (iteration/component/value traces, x/density predictive density, dim*/cluster MAP scatter, fitted/observed regression). Base graphics remains plotting dependency; users want ggplot2/lattice/plotly replot returned data. ppCheck()/posteriorPredict() now resolve monitored node’s columns per call instead per draw (memoised via attribute cache). Measured cost old lookup ~3e-05 s/call, clarity large-monitor-set improvement, speedup claim. Considered rejected: passing prior hyperparameters data nodes avoid recompilation prior changes. touch every family’s model code risks breaking NIMBLE’s conjugacy detection (FixedK regression fix showed already fragile dynamic indexing); compile cache already forces correct rebuild priors change.

what: Fixed: 65 tests silently erroring in installed-mode runs
title: nimix 1.2.0
text: Running suite installed package (opposed pkgload::load_all()) project’s guard documented bug class – kernels resolving load_all() R CMD INSTALL. guard blind spot: many tests called internal helpers (.rowPresence(), .nodeToArray(), .cacheKey(), buildConstants(), …) unqualified, works load_all() errors namespace exports attached – testthat counts errors, failures, summary reading “0 failed” hid . internal references tests now nimix:::-qualified, suite modes now report identically (588 passing, zero errors). Suite gates now check error flag, just failure count.

what: Improved: AF_slice defaults for correlated-parameter univariate families
title: nimix 1.2.0
text: nine 3-4 parameter univariate families (msnburr, msnburr2a, gmsnburr, fssn, fossep, fsst, jfst, sep, lep) now sample component’s parameters single automated-factor-slice block (Tibbits et al. 2014) instead independent univariate samplers. Motivation measured, assumed: fssn, cor(mu, alpha) reaches -0.94 default samplers delivered min ESS 12 1500 draws (0.28 ESS/sec); gmsnburr, min ESS 46 (0.86 ESS/sec). escalation ladder mattered – RW_block made fssn worse (min ESS 7) – AF_slice reached min ESS 622 (fssn, x32 ESS/sec) 417 (gmsnburr, x5.7), parameter recovery unchanged. Verified three engines (FixedK, DPM, MRF).

what: New: bayesplot interoperability – drawsArray() and ppcData()
title: nimix 1.2.0
text: drawsArray(fit) returns plain iterations x chains x parameters array – layout bayesplot::mcmc_* functions accept natively – ppcData(fit) returns list(y, yrep) bayesplot::ppc_dens_overlay() friends (margin argument multivariate fits). bayesplot enters Suggests ; adaptors return base R objects add runtime dependency. important part safety guard, plumbing: drawsArray(fit, "components") refuses serve per-component draws relabel(), explaining – label switching, muTilde[1] names different components different chains, R-hat computed raw trace looks valid means nothing. default "invariant" view (cluster count, allocation entropy, alpha) safe raw draws keeps true per-chain structure. relabelling, chain dimension honestly collapsed 1, conditioning modal cluster count leaves chains unequal lengths.

what: New: interoperability foundations – yrep and chain identity
title: nimix 1.2.0
text: posteriorPredict(fit, ndraws) returns posterior predictive replicates (ndraws x n, ndraws x n x d multivariate fits), ppCheck(..., store_yrep = TRUE) attaches yrep/y/draws attributes. Previously replicates computed discarded, made graphical PPC (e.g. bayesplot::ppc_dens_overlay(y, yrep)) impossible drive nimix fit. Storage stays opt-default result remains lean. Multi-chain fits now record diagnostics$chainId, marking chain pooled draw came . Post-hoc per-chain diagnostics (R-hat invariant functionals, per-chain traces, draws arrays) previously impossible reconstruct chains stacked without marker. Stored diagnostics list, FitResult class unchanged existing objects remain valid.

what: New: label-free partition summaries – psm() and binderPartition()
title: nimix 1.2.0
text: psm(fit) returns posterior similarity matrix P(z_i = z_j | y); binderPartition(fit) selects, among partitions chain actually visited, one minimising expected Binder loss (Dahl’s least-squares criterion). invariant label permutations number occupied clusters, every draw contributes – unlike relabel(), must condition modal cluster count align component parameters (measured: 34% DPM draws discarded two-cluster example). complements, replacements: relabel() answers “component parameters”, psm()/binderPartition() answer “observations belong together”. overlapping clusters similarity matrix expresses genuine allocation uncertainty (mid-region pairs ~0.65) instead forcing hard 0/1 answer.

what: Fixed: multivariate posterior predictive checks with empty components
title: nimix 1.2.0
text: ppCheck() normal-mv (inheriting student-t-mv, normal-gamma-mv) reconstructed draw’s covariance array dim = c(max(alloc), d, d). draws component empty, max(alloc) undercounts monitored components, truncating shifting covariance entries – usually chol() error, occasionally shifted-yet-still-PD matrix returning wrong replicates silently. dimension now derived monitored component count, defensive length check. Regression tests deliberately -fit K empty components must occur; six skew-mv families audited never affected.

what: Fixed: FixedK mixture regression is now scale-equivariant
title: nimix 1.2.0
text: FixedK engine, NIMBLE’s conjugacy checker see dynamically indexed betaTilde[z[], ], betaTilde/s2Tilde fell back adaptive random-walk samplers raw units. poorly scaled predictors biased fit visibly (slope 2.50 vs true 2.0 X multiplied 1000 – systematic, mixing). conjugate Normal-Inverse-Gamma Gibbs sampler now replaces FixedK path (DPM path already conjugate via CRP_cluster_wrapper). Measured: max slope discrepancy 1000x predictor rescale fell 0.48 0.004, minimum ESS/second coefficients rose ~35x (0.4 15.3). scale-equivariance test locks guarantee.

what: New: estimating the orthogonal factor O beyond two dimensions
title: nimix 1.1.0
text: six skew multivariate families now run finite-K, DPM MRF engines. Budget MCMC iterations general-m variants: component carries m(m-1)/2 slice-sampled angles, slows Potts sweep – 8x8 grid m = 3 heavy tails, 1200 iterations recovered spatial regions poorly 3000 recovered exactly. distribution = "skewistudent-mv-o" likewise accepts m >= 2. canonicalisation carries nu permutation never inverts : sign flip inverts gamma leaves nu alone, Student kernel symmetric. Density invariance checked 1e-15 m = 2, 3, 4. distribution = "skewnormal-mv-o" now accepts m >= 2. routes data dimension: m = 2 keeps dedicated implementation, m > 2 uses general Householder parameterisation m(m-1)/2 angles (FS Lemma 2). New exported helpers orthogonalFactor() canonicaliseO(). Restriction (8) canonicalisation, sampling constraint. FS write confining angles box Theta^j puts O O_m; testing directly, fraction box draws satisfy (8) 0.245 (m = 2), 0.069 (m = 3) 0.007 (m = 4). Constraining sampler 0.7% slice prior mix badly. true, nimix uses: among signed row permutations P |P| = +1, exactly one PO satisfies – verified exhaustively m = 2, 3, 4. angles sampled unconstrained posterior draw mapped unique representative, gamma carried along (gamma_i -> gamma_perm() reciprocal, per row sign). density invariant map, 1e-14. m! 2^m row ambiguity label switching dimension index, package already prefers post-hoc relabelling ordering constraints. Reassuringly, m = 2 (8)-satisfying set exactly theta (-pi/8, pi/8) – prior support already shipped. general treatment reduces bivariate one rather replacing . Experimental, read care. gamma O reported canonicalisation, comparing simulating values requires canonicalising . O_mean elementwise posterior mean orthogonal. mirror modes angle likelihood multiply m: partition location recovery robust (accuracy 1.0 m = 3), individual angles large-sample quantities.

what: New: estimating the orthogonal factor O (bivariate)
title: nimix 1.1.0
text: four skew multivariate families – fixed-O estimated-O, Gaussian heavy-tailed – run finite-K, DPM MRF engines. distribution = "skewistudent-mv-o": heavy-tailed counterpart, O estimated way. theta better identified Gaussian one, reason instructive: gamma = 1 skew-Normal density theta-invariant, spherical Normal errors carry directional information (FS Lemma 1). Independent Student margins spherical, skew-IStudent density depends theta even symmetry – verified numerically, profile likelihood recovering theta symmetric data. Letting nu -> Inf restores sphericity invariance, exactly theory predicts. Grid-initialisation theta uses family’s density. Initialising heavy-tailed family Gaussian profile picks wrong angle, outliers dominate Gaussian fit – observed, assumed. distribution = "skewnormal-mv-o": FS skew multivariate Normal orthogonal factor = OU estimated rather held fixed, via Householder angle theta (FS 2007, Appendix ). lifts main scope limitation skewnormal-mv: O determines directions asymmetry (FS Sec 3.3). Bivariate data now. theta uniform prior (-pi/8, pi/8), exactly FS’s identifiability restriction (8) written Householder parameterisation (O11 = cos 2 theta, O21 = -sin 2 theta, |O| = -1). |O| = -1 always, O = member FS’s restricted set: theta = 0 gives O = diag(1, -1), coincides skewnormal-mv replacing gamma_2 1/gamma_2. fixed-O family therefore nested theta = 0, reflection. Read theta care. gamma = 1 density depend theta , theta identified skewness. Even clear skewness likelihood near-mirror secondary mode: 150 observations per component sat within 1.65 log-likelihood units true mode (log-likelihood true parameters) chains settled wrong sign; 500 per component 95% intervals covered simulating angles. theta slice-sampled grid-initialised, treated large-sample quantity.

title: nimix 1.1.0
text: skew multivariate families run three engines: finite-K, DPM MRF (spatial Potts prior). * distribution = "skewistudent-mv": FS skew multivariate independent-Student (Ferreira & Steel 2007, Sec 5.2) – FS-skew Student-t margins per-dimension degrees freedom nu (stochastic, truncated 2), = chol(Sigma) construction harmonised gamma convention skewnormal-mv. Closed-form (lambda augmentation), avoids documented 25-38x partition-mixing penalty augmented skew-Student path model supported data FS’s application. Validated: kernel equals R reference 1e-15, nu -> Inf recovers skewnormal-mv, m = 1 equals univariate fsst, density integrates one, mixtures recover location, per-dimension skew direction partition (accuracy 1.0; DPM modal K correct). distribution = "skewnormal-mv": skewed multivariate distribution Ferreira & Steel (2007), eta = ' eps + mu, independent FS-skew-Normal margins eps, per-dimension skewness gamma (harmonised convention: gamma_j > 1 skews dimension j right; gamma = 1 recovers dmnorm exactly), = chol(Sigma) upper triangular Sigma ~ inverse-Wishart. Available finite-K DPM engines, dskewmvn() / rskewmvn() ppCheck() support. Validated: compiled kernel equals R reference 1e-14, 2-D density integrates one, m = 1 case equals univariate fssn, mixtures recover location, per-dimension skew direction, partition (accuracy 1.0; DPM modal K correct). Scope, stated plainly: orthogonal factor O = OU (FS Lemma 1) fixed identity. FS Sec 3.3 explains skewness O determines directions asymmetry, release ties directions coordinate axes triangular transform. Householder-parameterised O (FS Appendix ) planned follow-, skew multivariate independent-Student family.

what: Breaking change: one skewness convention across all Fernandez-Steel families
title: nimix 1.1.0
text: Previously fssn fsst parameterised skewness alpha = 1/gamma, fossep used alpha = gamma. alpha = 2 therefore skewed left fssn/fsst right fossep. Fits simulations internally consistent within family, never produced wrong inference, made families incomparable parameter uninterpretable across . Fernandez-Steel families (fssn, fsst, fossep) now share convention Fernandez & Steel (1998, 2007): exported alpha FS skewness gamma, alpha = 1 symmetric, alpha > 1 skews right, alpha < 1 skews left, P(X > mu) = alpha^2 / (1 + alpha^2) exactly, every family. means . fssn fsst results computed 1.0.1 earlier correspond reciprocal alpha 1.1.0: estimate alpha = 2 alpha = 0.5 now. Densities, CDFs, quantiles, RNG compiled NIMBLE kernels updated together, regression test (test-skew-convention.R) now pins guarantee. fossep unchanged. Jones-Faddy family (jfst) uses alpha/theta shapes affected; neither MSNBurr families.

what: Batch B: six new neo-normal component families
title: nimix 1.0.1
text: six univariate, non-conjugate, available finite-K, DPM MRF engines, d/p/q/r functions ppCheck() support. NIMBLE kernel built registered global environment (pattern required scalar user-defined densities) validated R reference 1e-8, density integrating one family’s known reduction reproduced exactly. distribution = "sep" – symmetric exponential power (nu = 2 Normal, nu = 1 Laplace). distribution = "lep" – exponential power alternative parameterisation (nu = 2 Normal). distribution = "fssn" – Fernandez-Steel skew Normal (alpha = 1 Normal), log-normal prior alpha treats left/right skew symmetrically. distribution = "fossep" – Fernandez-Steel skew exponential power (theta = 2 skew-Normal kernel). distribution = "fsst" – Fernandez-Steel skew Student-t (alpha = 1 symmetric-t). nu stochastic node truncated 2 variance exists; weakly identified data. t-kernel inlined rather calling NIMBLE’s dt. distribution = "jfst" – Jones-Faddy skew-t (alpha = theta symmetric). density uses branch-free identity sign(z)/sqrt((+th)/z^2 + 1) == z/sqrt(+ th + z^2), also finite z = 0. q* functions families recycle vector parameters sample length subset per branch, per-observation parameter vectors (used posterior predictive simulation) align without recycling warnings.

title: nimix 1.0.0 (in development)
text: MRF engine: Potts prior’s dPottsNimix / rPottsNimix now built registered global environment (like scalar neo-normal densities). Registering package namespace made NIMBLE fail find rPottsNimix code generation latent label node package installed (library(nimix)), every MRF fit errored normal install working load_all(). Fixed. ppCheck() MSNBurr / MSNBurr-IIa / GMSNBurr longer emits recycling warnings: quantile functions now recycle vector parameters sample length index interior subset consistently, per-observation parameter vectors (used posterior predictive simulation) align. Multivariate cluster summary() columns now mu_<j>_mean (_med / _lwr / _upr) consistency univariate summaries.

title: nimix 1.0.0 (in development)
text: two shape parameters alpha theta. theta = 1 recovers MSNBurr, alpha = 1 recovers MSNBurr-IIa, alpha = theta symmetric, alpha = theta -> inf converges Normal. Available three engines (finite-K, DPM, MRF), numerically stable (NIMBLE density matches R reference 1e-8; density integrates one; exact reduction MSNBurr / MSNBurr-IIa verified). d/p/q/r functions ppCheck() support included.

title: nimix 1.0.0 (in development)
text: mcmcControl gains parallel = TRUE (nchains > 1) run chains parallel via parallel::mclapply. worker builds compiles model separate directory – fork-safe way parallelise NIMBLE, avoiding shared-C++-object temp-directory collisions naive mclapply hit. Forking (Unix/macOS; Windows falls back sequential); ncores caps worker count. Verified recover label-invariant summary sequential path.

what: Bug fix: robust source load order
title: nimix 1.0.0 (in development)
text: Added @include directives across S4 class spec files package’s Collate order derived topologically actual class dependencies. fixes load failure (undefined slot classes definition "MRFEngine" / definition found superclass "NormalRegSpec") occur whenever files sourced alphabetical rather Collate order.

what: Cluster profiling, richer summaries, model selection and ensembling
title: nimix 1.0.0 (in development)
text: summary() relabelled clustering fit now reports posterior median alongside mean 95% credible interval every scalar component parameter (univariate families) per dimension multivariate clustering. New clusterProfile(): assigns observation MAP cluster describes observed data within cluster (size, proportion, per-variable mean / sd / median) – data-side complement summary() (reports fitted parameters) plot(fit, "cluster") (shows partition). New predictive model-selection layer built label-invariant pointwise mixture log-likelihood: nimixWAIC() (native; Watanabe 2010), nimixLOO() (PSIS-LOO via loo package; Vehtari, Gelman & Gabry 2017), modelSelect() rank several fits (e.g. choosing K, comparing Normal / Student-t / MSNBurr components data). New ensembleFit(): combines several fits one weighted predictive model via Bayesian stacking Pseudo-BMA+ (Yao et al. 2018, needs loo) Akaike-style WAIC weights (native). predict() returns ensemble-weighted density. loo added Suggests.

title: nimix 1.0.0 (in development)
text: Two new univariate component families, distribution = "msnburr" "msnburr2a" (Iriawan 2000; Choir 2020), location, scale, skewness shape alpha (alpha = 1 exactly logistic distribution; MSNBurr accommodates left skew, MSNBurr-IIa mirror). Available three engines: finite-K, DPM, spatial MRF. Numerically stable throughout, using maintainer-contributed reference implementation: asymptotic log-omega branch alpha -> 0, branch-free softplus compiled densities, two-branch quantile inversion. Log-densities stay finite standardized values hundreds; density integrates one alpha = 0.05 alpha = 100; NIMBLE R densities agree 1e-8; ppCheck() supports families. Verified recovery synthetic skew-component mixtures (finite-K allocation accuracy 1.00 / 0.99, DPM recovers true K, MRF accuracy 1.00).

what: Bayesian workflow: modern mixture-appropriate convergence + predictive checks
title: nimix 1.0.0 (in development)
text: Full Vehtari et al. (2021) convergence suite – rank-normalized split-Rhat (already present), folded split-Rhat, bulk-ESS tail-ESS – computed label-invariant functionals (occupied K, DPM alpha, MRF beta, new per-iteration allocation entropy): per-component traces identified label switching, chain diagnostics meaningless. summary() prints functional table documented Rhat < 1.01 aim; helpers unit-tested theory (iid ESS ~ n, folded Rhat detects pure scale disagreement location Rhat misses, AR(0.9) ESS matches analytic value). New ppCheck(): posterior predictive checks (Gelman, Meng & Stern 1996; Gelman et al. 2020, Bayesian workflow) replicates drawn conditionally draw’s fitted allocation – label-invariant construction valid every engine including spatial MRF. Built-statistics (mean, sd, min, max, skew) user functions; clustering families (Gaussian uv/mv, Student-t, Normal-Gamma, Poisson, Binomial) supported, regression checks planned. Verified pass well-specified Gaussian mixture flag Poisson mixture overdispersed counts (tail p = 0.013).

what: MRF engine across the distribution registry (batch 2) – matrix complete
title: nimix 0.9.0
text: method = "mrf" now covers every registered family: added multivariate Student-t clustering, multivariate-response regression (Gaussian, Student-t Normal-Gamma responses), augmented Normal-Gamma routes (univariate/multivariate clustering univariate regression), O(nK + edges) label sweep. Harness groups mrfdist2 mrfdist2reg pass synthetic lattice (7 combinations x 3 seeds). Critical correctness fix. hand-written MRF kernels Gaussian specs shadowed generic .pottsify() route heavy-tailed subclasses (S4 inheritance), e.g. Student-t regression mrf silently used Gaussian kernel parameter updates. hand-written kernels removed: every family’s MRF kernel now derived fixed-K kernel, regression test pins kernel/family correspondence. internal quiet-mode handler crashed NIMBLE conditions empty messages (“argument length zero”); now robust, benign “samplers assigned” notice deliberately unsampled fixed beta node muffled. Mixing benchmark (documented commitment): identical heavy-tailed lattice data, augmented Normal-Gamma route mixes component means BETTER direct Student-t density MRF (ESS minimum component mean 1357 vs 550 equal wall time) – reverse exchangeable-DPM finding, K fixed labels pinned spatial field omega-partition coupling penalty largely disappears conjugate Gibbs updates win. Guidance: prefer normalgamma MRF parameter mixing; direct-t route remains available equally valid.

what: MRF engine across the distribution registry (batch 1: closed-form emissions)
title: nimix 0.8.0
text: Per maintainer-approved feasibility study, method = "mrf" now covers six family/task combinations: Poisson Binomial clustering, Poisson-GLM (log link) Binomial-GLM (logit link) regression, direct Student-t clustering regression. Every DSL emission call verified empirically reference densities implementation. New internal .pottsify() transformation derives family’s MRF kernel mechanically fixed-K kernel (drop Dirichlet-categorical label layer, insert joint Potts node), default buildModelCode(<spec>, MRFEngine) now exists; three original hand-written MRF kernels unchanged. Label sweeps remain family-specific O(nK + edges) performance. method = "mrf" now requires K >= 2 (one-state Potts field degenerate). augmented Normal-Gamma routes remain explicitly blocked pending batch 2 (planned mixing benchmark direct-t routes). Recovery harness gains mrfdist1 mrfdist1reg groups: six new combinations pass synthetic two-block lattice across 3 seeds (parameter recovery + allocation accuracy 0.98-1.00 verification runs).

what: Spatial line hardening + official-statistics case study
title: nimix 0.8.0
text: usStateAdj (state contiguity matrix derived Census Bureau’s 2023 county adjacency file); full provenance help pages. New vignette spatial-mixture.Rmd: 2023 SAIPE poverty rates MRF engine (estimated interaction, posterior mean ~1.2) finds two regimes whose high-poverty regime spatially contiguous Southern belt (AL AR DC FL GA LA MS NC NM OK SC TN TX) – structure exchangeable mixture represent; plain fixedk fit agrees 82% states. spatial regression example honestly collapses single national income-poverty regime. recovery harness gains mrfbeta group (pseudo-likelihood beta estimation synthetic lattice, 3 seeds).

what: MRF engine: Bayesian estimation of the interaction beta
title: nimix 0.7.0
text: prior$estimateBeta = TRUE (optional prior$betaMax, default 2) puts uniform prior Potts interaction updates random-walk Metropolis Besag (1975) pseudo-likelihood – classical approximate route hidden Potts fields, since exact posterior beta doubly intractable. documented approximation; exchange-algorithm refinement (Murray, Ghahramani & MacKay 2006) possible future upgrade. beta now model node modes; estimateBeta = FALSE (default) sampler touches , reproducing fixed-beta behaviour exactly. Correctness guards: NIMBLE’s default sampler beta (target unnormalised Potts density – wrong beta) removed unconditionally, compiled-model cache now distinguishes estimation fixed mode (compile different sampler sets). MRF-specific diagnostics: summary() reports split-Rhat / ESS posterior mean beta estimated, engine warns posterior piles near betaMax (near-saturated field). synthetic two-block lattice, estimated interaction concentrates well zero (posterior mean ~1.6, P(beta > 0.2) = 1) perfect block recovery, fixed-beta path unchanged.

title: nimix 0.7.0
text: nimixReg() gains method = "mrf" spatialWeights argument: mixture Gaussian linear regressions whose latent regime labels follow Potts field neighbourhood graph – regression coefficients cluster spatially (e.g. growth patterns across adjacent regions). fixed prior$beta interaction clustering engine. synthetic two-block lattice opposite slopes (+2 / -2) heavy noise, spatial smoothing lifts regime-allocation accuracy 0.867 (fixedk) 1.000 recovering slopes. recovery harness gains mrfreg group (3 seeds). Heavy-tailed regression responses explicitly blocked mrf clear message (inherit Gaussian regression spec otherwise use wrong emission density).

title: nimix 0.7.0
text: method = "mrf" now also accepts multivariate data: multivariate Gaussian components Normal-Inverse-Wishart kernel, label sweep evaluating dmnorm_chol per component (Cholesky factors hoisted per sweep). synthetic two-block lattice spatial smoothing lifts multivariate allocation accuracy 0.942 (fixedk) 0.992. Sampler selection fully polymorphic (S4 dispatch component spec); heavy-tailed families inherit Gaussian specs explicitly blocked clear message instead silently using wrong emission density.

what: Spatially constrained mixtures: the MRF engine
title: nimix 0.6.0
text: New method = "mrf" nimixClust(): finite mixture whose latent labels follow Potts Markov random field spatialWeights neighbourhood (Potts 1952; Besag 1974; spatially variant finite mixtures, Blekas et al. 2005), neighbouring observations favour component. Requires known K SpatialWeightSpec; interaction strength fixed prior$beta (default 0.8; beta = 0 removes smoothing). Univariate Gaussian components release. Implementation: (intentionally unnormalised) user-defined NIMBLE Potts distribution – exact MCMC beta fixed, intractable partition function constant – plus custom single-site Gibbs sweep sampler labels; theta updates remain conjugate. Bayesian estimation beta planned later 1.x release. synthetic two-block lattice overlapping components, spatial smoothing lifts allocation accuracy 0.883 (fixedk, smoothing) 0.967, beta = 0 reproduces unsmoothed behaviour. recovery harness gains mrf group (3 seeds, passing). Opens post-1.0 spatial line: spatially constrained mixtures latent component labels follow Markov random field neighbourhood graph (Besag 1974; spatially variant finite mixtures, Blekas et al. 2005). New S4 class SpatialWeightSpec: validated neighbourhood structure (symmetric, zero-diagonal, non-negative weight matrix) deliberately orthogonal DistributionSpec, registered component family can paired graph. Constructors spatialWeights() (matrix) gridAdjacency() (rook/queen contiguity regular lattice); accessors nRegions(), getAdjacency(), neighborsOf(). nimixClust() gains spatialWeights argument (default NULL, fully backward-compatible). Supplying structure validates points MRF engine planned 0.6.0; exchangeable mixture remains default behaviour. First stable release. public API – nimixClust(), nimixReg(), registerDistribution(), relabel(), summary(), plot(), predict(), nimixClearCache() – now considered stable; breaking changes bump major version.

title: nimix 0.6.0
text: New packaged dataset wdi2022: four development indicators 207 countries (World Bank World Development Indicators, 2022; CC 4.0; full provenance ?wdi2022). four vignettes now run official-statistics data instead simulations : income-regime clustering (univariate), joint income-longevity clustering (multivariate), Preston curve mixture regressions (instructive single-regime result: DPM invent components), Student-t vs Normal-Gamma heavy-tail comparison. Reported numbers vignettes come actual runs.

title: nimix 0.5.0
text: line opens performance hardening phase, built 0.4.3 feature set. two production engines method = "dpm" (Dirichlet process / Chinese restaurant process; number occupied components estimated) method = "fixedk" (finite mixture known K).

title: nimix 0.5.0
text: Compile-/ reuse. fit reuses compiled NIMBLE model (MCMC) later fit identical structure – generated code, constants, monitors component family – resetting data initial values. skips recompilation repeated fits (multiple seeds, multiple chains) bit--bit identical fresh compile. Controlled mcmcControl$reuse (default TRUE); nimixClearCache() releases cached compiled models. Reproducibility. dispersed (k-means) initialisation now seeded fit’s seed, repeated fits data seed coincide exactly. caller’s global random stream left untouched. Multi-chain + convergence diagnostics. mcmcControl$nchains (default 1) runs several chains dispersed, separately seeded starts, reusing compiled model first chain compiles. summary() reports rank-normalized split-Rhat effective sample size label-invariant quantities (occupied-cluster count, DPM concentration), warns Rhat exceeds 1.1. Per-component parameters deliberately excluded Rhat avoid label-switching artefacts. Vectorised post-processing. per-draw occupied-cluster count, allocation-trace parsing, relabelling recode/weight steps now vectorised (single tabulate/matrix-index passes instead per-row loops). Verified bit--bit identical previous implementation; remaining relabelling cost external ECR algorithm . Hardening harness. inst/harness/run_recovery_suite.R now systematic distribution x engine recovery matrix: every released clustering family (Gaussian, Student-t, Normal-Gamma – univariate multivariate – Poisson, Binomial) regression path fitted engines data known truth, across three MCMC seeds (18 combinations, 54 fits). DPM discrete counts recovery criterion assesses dominant components (weight >= 0.1), reflecting known diffuseness DPM posterior number components data (Miller & Harrison, 2013). Previously untested engine pairings additionally pinned tests/testthat/test-hardening-matrix.R.

title: nimix 0.4.3
text: verbose now defaults FALSE nimixClust() nimixReg(). Benign NIMBLE configuration chatter (e.g. Chinese-restaurant-process truncation reminder) muffled selectively; genuine warnings potentially invalid MCMC draws errors always propagate, verbose modes. Dispersed k-means initialisation now headroom-aware: number seeded clusters respects truncation level (K_max) early CRP transients longer breach smaller K_max. initialisation headroom configurable mcmcControl$initRatio (default 0.8). Values leave little headroom (>= 0.95) warn; values outside (0, 1) error.

title: nimix 0.4.3
text: Two production engines: method = "dpm" method = "fixedk", available univariate multivariate clustering regression (including multivariate responses). Component distributions: Gaussian (univariate / multivariate), Student-t Normal-Gamma (heavy-tailed, univariate / multivariate), Poisson / Binomial counts.

title: nimix 0.4.2
text: nimixReg() gains multivariate responses (cbind(y1, y2) ~ x) Normal, Student-t Normal-Gamma components, per-component coefficient matrices error covariances.

title: nimix 0.4.0
text: Student-t Normal-Gamma components (univariate multivariate) Poisson / Binomial counts; public registerDistribution().

title: nimix 0.3.0
text: nimixReg() RegressionMixModel class: mixture--regressions Normal-Inverse-Gamma g-prior. FixedKEngine implemented across univariate, multivariate regression models. Engine selection polymorphic via runEngine().

title: nimix 0.2.0
text: Multivariate Gaussian clustering (NormalMvSpec) Normal-Inverse-Wishart base measure. Engine generalised dimension-agnostic.

title: nimix 0.1.0
text: S4 foundation, univariate Gaussian clustering (NormalUvSpec), nimixClust() DPM fixed-K engines.
